head: 1769a43
method: Phase G methodology unchanged: pg_ctl restart -m fast clears shared_buffers + per-backend caches, then 50 fresh psql sessions each issue one EXPLAIN (ANALYZE) k=10 NN query (search_k=100, tv_4bit). Warm sweep is 50 queries in a single primed session.

configs.label: tv_4bit_k100_cold
configs.note: First fresh-backend query (qid=1) was 14.6 s (shared_buffers also cold); subsequent fresh backends ran 6.6-7.4 s with shared_buffers warm but per-backend Arc<IdMapIndex> cache cold.

configs.label: tv_4bit_k100
configs.note: warm-cache control; matches Phase G to within 1 ms p50 as expected (warm path skips read_idmap_from via cache hit).

comparison.interpretation: No measurable speedup. The vendor patch eliminated the tmpfile round-trip but cold cost on this corpus is dominated by SPI fetch of the ~195 MiB bytea payload (PG TOAST decompression + detoasting) plus HashMap<u64, usize> construction for slot_to_id (1M entries). Both happen whether the deserialiser reads from a tmpfile or directly from a &[u8]. Closing the cold-path gap will require a relfile-resident page format (cached in shared_buffers) instead of a single bytea heap row.
